content.text: Cross-Functional PRR Sign-Off Requirements

A Production Readiness Review is only as strong as the stakeholders who sign it. Collecting a list of checkboxes from the engineering team alone leaves critical gaps: security blind spots that only a dedicated security engineer catches, cost surprises that finance discovers after launch, and legal exposure that product or compliance teams flag too late. A cross-functional sign-off process distributes the review across the people who are accountable for each risk domain.

For GenAI services, the stakeholder set is wider than for conventional services. ML engineers must attest to eval coverage. FinOps must review token cost projections. Legal or compliance must assess output liability for regulated industries. Each stakeholder has a defined role — Approver, Reviewer, or Informed — and a deadline before which their sign-off must be recorded in the launch ticket.

attrs.alt: Cross-functional PRR sign-off matrix showing stakeholder roles across domains

content.text: Stakeholder Roles and Responsibilities

The RACI model (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed) is the standard framework for assigning sign-off roles. For a PRR, collapse it to three levels: Approver (their sign-off is required to proceed — blocking), Reviewer (they must review and raise objections before sign-off, but their approval is not independently blocking), and Informed (they receive the PRR report but are not required to act).

## PRR Sign-Off Roles

### SRE / Platform Engineering — HARD BLOCKER
Approves: Reliability domain (SLOs, retries, circuit breakers, on-call coverage)
Reviews:  Security domain (network policies, secret hygiene)
Deadline: 5 business days before launch

Required evidence:
- [ ] SLO targets defined in config/slos.yaml and linked to Prometheus alerts
- [ ] Load test results showing P99 latency ≤ SLO target at 2x expected traffic
- [ ] Runbook linked from the service's PagerDuty escalation policy
- [ ] On-call rotation includes at least two engineers familiar with the LLM stack

### Security Engineering — HARD BLOCKER
Approves: Security domain (secrets management, prompt injection mitigations, output validation)
Deadline: 7 business days before launch (longest lead time — start early)

Required evidence:
- [ ] Threat model documented (even a brief one covering prompt injection, data exfiltration)
- [ ] No hardcoded API keys (prr_security_check.py passes clean)
- [ ] Output validator in place and tested for PII leakage and jailbreak outputs
- [ ] Secrets stored in Vault with preview/prod path separation

### ML Engineer — HARD BLOCKER
Approves: Quality/Eval domain (eval suite coverage, pass rate, model version pin)
Reviews:  Reliability (timeout/fallback), Security (prompt design)
Deadline: 5 business days before launch

Required evidence:
- [ ] Eval suite covers ≥80% of known user intent categories
- [ ] Full eval run passes at ≥95% on the production model + prompt version
- [ ] Model version pinned in config/model-config.prod.yaml (no "latest" alias)
- [ ] Shadow eval scheduled to run in production post-launch

### FinOps / Finance — HARD BLOCKER
Approves: Cost domain (token caps, budget alerts, killswitch configured)
Deadline: 3 business days before launch

Required evidence:
- [ ] Daily token budget and per-user cap configured and tested
- [ ] Cost projection at P50 and P99 traffic documented in launch ticket
- [ ] CloudWatch/Datadog budget alert set at 80% of approved daily spend
- [ ] Feature-flag killswitch tested end-to-end (stub responds, real calls stop)

### Product / Legal — SOFT BLOCKER
Reviews:  Quality (output appropriateness for user population), Security (data retention)
Deadline: 10 business days before launch

Required evidence:
- [ ] Output content policy reviewed and approved for target user population
- [ ] Data retention policy for conversation logs documented and compliant with ToS
- [ ] Disclosure to users that responses are AI-generated (if required by regulation)

### Engineering Lead — FINAL GATE
Reviews:  All domains — last sign-off after all others are green
Deadline: 1 business day before launch

The engineering lead signs off that all blockers are resolved and the PRR is complete.
They do not re-audit each domain — they verify that all required sign-offs are recorded.
Tracking Sign-Offs in a Launch Ticket

Record all PRR sign-offs in a structured field in the launch ticket (Jira, Linear, or GitHub Issue), not in a Slack thread. Slack threads are ephemeral — the sign-off record must survive the conversation. The ticket should have a dedicated PRR checklist section with one checkbox per required sign-off, and each checkbox links to the evidence artifact (test run URL, report file, or comment timestamp).

## PRR Sign-Off Tracker — genai-chat-service v1.0

**Target launch date:** 2026-08-01
**PRR coordinator:** @alice

| Domain        | Approver          | Status      | Evidence link                          | Sign-off date |
|---------------|-------------------|-------------|----------------------------------------|---------------|
| Reliability   | @sre-bob          | ✅ APPROVED  | [Load test #4872](https://ci.example/) | 2026-07-25    |
| Security      | @sec-carol        | ✅ APPROVED  | [SecReview-2026-07-20](https://...)    | 2026-07-24    |
| Quality/Eval  | @ml-dave          | ✅ APPROVED  | [Eval run #312](https://eval.example/) | 2026-07-25    |
| Cost          | @finops-erin      | ⏳ PENDING   | Awaiting P99 cost projection           | —             |
| Legal/Product | @product-frank    | ✅ APPROVED  | Comment #42 on this ticket             | 2026-07-22    |
| Engineering   | @lead-grace       | 🔴 BLOCKED   | Waiting for FinOps sign-off            | —             |

**Launch is blocked until:** Cost domain approved by @finops-erin

Assign a PRR coordinator (typically the tech lead for the service) who owns the ticket, chases outstanding sign-offs, and escalates to the engineering manager if any sign-off is at risk of missing the deadline. The coordinator does not approve any domain — they manage the process.
Automating Sign-Off Collection

Manual ticket updates drift. A bot that reads the CI artifact store and automatically marks checklist items as green when the corresponding script passes removes the coordination overhead and makes the PRR audit trail machine-readable.

# scripts/prr_status_bot.py
# Called by CI after each domain check passes.
# Updates a structured JSON status file that the launch ticket links to.
# Requires: pip install python-dateutil

import json, sys, datetime, pathlib

STATUS_FILE = pathlib.Path("prr-status.json")

def load_status() -> dict:
    if STATUS_FILE.exists():
        return json.loads(STATUS_FILE.read_text())
    return {
        "service": "",
        "version": "",
        "domains": {},
        "all_hard_blockers_green": False,
        "launch_approved": False,
    }

def record_domain_pass(domain: str, evidence_url: str, approver: str) -> None:
    status = load_status()
    status["domains"][domain] = {
        "status": "PASS",
        "approver": approver,
        "evidence_url": evidence_url,
        "timestamp": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
    }
    hard_blockers = ["reliability", "security", "quality_eval", "cost"]
    status["all_hard_blockers_green"] = all(
        status["domains"].get(d, {}).get("status") == "PASS"
        for d in hard_blockers
    )
    STATUS_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(status, indent=2))
    print(f"PRR domain '{domain}' recorded as PASS by {approver}")
    print(f"All hard blockers green: {status['all_hard_blockers_green']}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    # Usage: python prr_status_bot.py <domain> <evidence_url> <approver>
    domain, evidence_url, approver = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]
    record_domain_pass(domain, evidence_url, approver)

# .github/workflows/prr-gate.yml
# Final gate: blocks merge to main unless all hard blockers are green.
name: PRR Gate

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    paths:
      - "config/model-config.prod.yaml"
      - "prompts/**"

jobs:
  check-prr-status:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Verify PRR sign-offs
        run: |
          if [ ! -f prr-status.json ]; then
            echo "ERROR: prr-status.json not found. Run the PRR process before merging to main."
            exit 1
          fi

          ALL_GREEN=$(python3 -c "
          import json, sys
          s = json.load(open('prr-status.json'))
          print(str(s.get('all_hard_blockers_green', False)).lower())
          ")

          if [ "$ALL_GREEN" != "true" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: Not all hard-blocker PRR domains are green."
            python3 -c "
          import json
          s = json.load(open('prr-status.json'))
          for d, v in s['domains'].items():
              print(f'  {d}: {v["status"]}')
            "
            exit 1
          fi

          echo "PRR gate: all hard blockers PASS. Merge permitted."
Handling Exceptions and Waivers

Occasionally a hard blocker cannot be resolved before a business-critical launch date — a known security gap with a mitigation plan, or an eval suite that is 93% rather than 95%. These cases require a formal waiver, not a silent bypass. A waiver must document the gap, the risk acceptance rationale, the mitigating controls in place, and a time-bound remediation commitment.

## PRR Waiver — genai-chat-service v1.0

**Domain:** Quality / Eval
**Waiver requested by:** @ml-dave (ML Engineer)
**Waiver approved by:** @lead-grace (Engineering Lead) + @product-frank (Product)
**Date:** 2026-07-28

### Gap
Eval suite passes at 93.2% against the production model, below the 95% threshold.
The 6.8% failures are concentrated in edge cases for the "account cancellation" intent category.

### Risk Acceptance
Account cancellation queries represent <0.5% of production traffic.
Failures in this category degrade gracefully — the user is routed to a human agent.
No PII leakage or harmful output risk identified in the failing cases.

### Mitigating Controls
1. Human-agent fallback is active for all "account cancellation" intents at launch.
2. Shadow eval scheduled to run in production from day 1, alerting if pass rate drops below 90%.
3. Targeted eval improvement sprint scheduled for the 2-week window post-launch.

### Remediation Commitment
Eval pass rate will reach ≥95% within 14 calendar days of launch (by 2026-08-15).
If not met, the feature is gated behind a feature flag until the threshold is reached.

**Waiver expires:** 2026-08-15

A waiver is not a rubber stamp — it is a documented risk decision with a named approver and a hard expiry date. If the remediation commitment is not met by the waiver expiry, the engineering lead must gate the feature or escalate to the VP of Engineering.
Escalation Paths When Sign-Off Is At Risk

When a sign-off is likely to miss the deadline — the security review is dragging, or FinOps has questions that require a design meeting — the PRR coordinator must escalate proactively rather than letting the launch date slip silently. Each stakeholder group has a defined escalation path.

## Escalation Matrix

| Stakeholder   | Direct contact    | Escalation (1 business day)    | Further escalation (2 days) |
|---------------|-------------------|--------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| SRE           | SRE on-call lead  | SRE manager                    | VP Engineering              |
| Security Eng  | Security lead     | CISO / Security Director       | VP Engineering              |
| ML Engineer   | ML lead           | ML manager                     | VP Engineering              |
| FinOps        | FinOps analyst    | CFO delegate                   | VP Engineering + CFO        |
| Legal/Product | Product manager   | General counsel delegate       | CEO (for regulated markets) |

## Escalation Triggers

Escalate immediately (same day) if:
- A hard-blocker sign-off is still PENDING with < 2 business days to launch.
- A stakeholder has raised a blocker objection with no resolution path in sight.
- A waiver is needed but no approver has agreed to own the risk.

Escalate within 1 business day if:
- A hard-blocker sign-off is PENDING with < 5 business days to launch.
- A soft-blocker objection has not been addressed within 3 business days.

The escalation matrix must be included in the launch ticket at creation time — not assembled reactively on launch day. When the coordinator opens the PRR ticket, they paste in the relevant rows for each stakeholder group so the path is visible to everyone.

Never escalate over a stakeholder's head without first notifying them directly. A 'heads-up' Slack message saying 'I'm about to escalate to your manager because we're 2 days from launch and your sign-off is still open' is respectful and often resolves the sign-off faster than the escalation itself.
Post-Launch Monitoring Commitments

The PRR is not complete at launch — each approver makes a monitoring commitment that extends into the first 30 days of production operation. These commitments are recorded in the same launch ticket and reviewed at the post-launch retrospective.

## Post-Launch Monitoring Commitments

### SRE (reliability)
- [ ] Monitor SLO burn rate dashboard daily for the first 7 days.
- [ ] Review on-call incident volume at the end of week 1 and week 2.
- [ ] If P99 latency exceeds SLO target for > 30 minutes, page the ML engineer.

### ML Engineer (quality)
- [ ] Shadow eval results reviewed daily for the first 14 days.
- [ ] If quality score drops below 0.90, open a P1 incident immediately.
- [ ] Deliver the follow-up eval improvement sprint within 14 days of launch.

### FinOps (cost)
- [ ] Daily token spend reviewed for the first 7 days.
- [ ] If daily spend exceeds 120% of P99 projection, alert the engineering lead.
- [ ] Provide a 30-day cost actuals vs. projection report to the VP Engineering.

### Security Engineering (security)
- [ ] Review Vault audit logs for any unexpected path access in week 1.
- [ ] Confirm no hardcoded-key alerts fired in production (gitleaks, Vault).
- [ ] Schedule a 30-day security posture review for the service.

# scripts/post_launch_checkin.py
# Runs 7 days after launch and reports on post-launch monitoring commitments.
# Triggered by a scheduled GitHub Actions workflow.

import datetime, json, requests, os, sys

LAUNCH_DATE_ISO = os.environ["LAUNCH_DATE"]
SERVICE = os.environ["SERVICE_NAME"]
SLACK_WEBHOOK = os.environ.get("SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL")

launch_date = datetime.date.fromisoformat(LAUNCH_DATE_ISO)
today = datetime.date.today()
days_live = (today - launch_date).days

# Gather metrics from Prometheus / Datadog
def fetch_metric(query: str) -> float:
    """Stub: replace with actual Prometheus query."""
    return 0.0

p99_latency = fetch_metric('histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(llm_request_duration_seconds_bucket[24h]))')
quality_score = fetch_metric('llm_quality_score{eval_suite="prod"}')
daily_token_cost_usd = fetch_metric('sum(rate(llm_tokens_total[24h])) * 3600 * 24 * 0.00002')

report = {
    "service": SERVICE,
    "days_live": days_live,
    "p99_latency_s": round(p99_latency, 2),
    "quality_score": round(quality_score, 3),
    "daily_token_cost_usd": round(daily_token_cost_usd, 2),
    "alerts": [],
}

if p99_latency > 8.0:
    report["alerts"].append(f"WARN: P99 latency {p99_latency:.1f}s exceeds 8s threshold")
if quality_score < 0.90:
    report["alerts"].append(f"CRIT: Quality score {quality_score:.2f} below 0.90 floor")

print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))

if SLACK_WEBHOOK and report["alerts"]:
    msg = f"*Post-launch check ({days_live}d): {SERVICE}*\n" + "\n".join(f"• {a}" for a in report["alerts"])
    requests.post(SLACK_WEBHOOK, json={"text": msg})

if any("CRIT" in a for a in report["alerts"]):
    sys.exit(1)
Summary

Cross-functional PRR sign-off assigns five stakeholder groups to specific domains: SRE owns reliability, Security Engineering owns the security domain, ML Engineers own eval quality, FinOps owns cost controls, and Product/Legal reviews output appropriateness — each with a hard deadline before launch.

Hard blockers (SRE, Security, ML, FinOps) must all be APPROVED before the engineering lead grants the final gate. Soft blockers (Legal/Product) require documented objection resolution but do not independently block.
Track all sign-offs in a structured launch ticket field with evidence links — never in a Slack thread — so the audit trail survives the conversation.
Automate sign-off recording with prr_status_bot.py and enforce the final gate in CI via a prr-gate.yml workflow that blocks merges to main if all_hard_blockers_green is false.
Exceptions require a formal waiver with a named approver, documented risk rationale, mitigating controls, and a time-bound remediation commitment — not a silent bypass of the checklist.
Assign a PRR coordinator who owns the ticket and escalation path but approves no domain; their job is to ensure all sign-offs are collected before the launch-day deadline.
